description:
Summer internships in Rep. Angie Craig's office will be held in-person in the Washington, D.C. office. The D.C. internship will run approximately from May 26th, 2026, through Early August, with some room for flexibility. All interns are paid a stipend and provided with the necessary technology for their work. Schedules are flexible to accommodate course schedules and academic programs; however, a minimum availability of 20-25 hours per week is highly preferred.

All interns will have the opportunity to work with staff in both the D.C. and district offices over the course of the internship and the program is easily tailored to fit interns' policy interests and career goals. As a result, interns will learn about the legislative process, learn about all the different functions of a congressional office and gain hands-on professional experience while serving the constituents of Minnesota's Second Congressional District.
responsibilities: Work with staff in both the D.C. and district offices
Learn about the legislative process
Learn about all the different functions of a congressional office
Gain hands-on professional experience
Serve the constituents of Minnesota's Second Congressional District
qualifications: Passionate about public service
Confident and effective communicators
Proactive problem-solvers
Possess strong writing skills
Comfortable speaking on the phone and working with the general public
Minimum availability of 20-25 hours per week highly preferred
Ties to Minnesota (especially the Second Congressional District) encouraged
how_to_apply: To apply for an internship in the DC office, please email a combined PDF of your resume and one-page cover letter to craig.internships@mail.house.gov. Please format your email subject line as 'FirstName.LastName - DC Office Internship' to specify which office you are applying for. Please include your weekly availability between the hours of 9:00 AM and 6:00 PM in your cover letter and confirm that you will be available for in-person work. For all summer internships, applications must be submitted no later than 9 AM CT/10 AM ET on Wednesday, April 1st to craig.internships@mail.house.gov.
equal_opportunity: Rep. Craig's office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, age or protected veteran status.

description:
The Office of Congressman Gabe Vasquez seeks a part-time, temporary Communications Fellow for the summer (May through August, exact dates flexible). The Communications Fellow is an integral member of the Congressman's office and the communications team. Your role is essential to the office's overall mission of communicating the Congressman and his staff's work on behalf of New Mexicans to his constituents and the press.

This is a part-time, temporary position, with 20-25 hours of work per week expected. The schedule is flexible, and a hybrid remote and in-person work arrangement is preferred. Fully remote applicants will also be considered.
responsibilities: Drafting written materials including press releases, e-newsletters, social media content
Designing graphics
Creating short video clips as needed
Building press lists
qualifications: Excellent writing skills
Graphic design skills
Creativity
Attention to detail
Relevant prior experience
how_to_apply: To be considered, please send a resume, cover letter, writing sample, and two digital samples with the subject line "NM-02 Spring Communications Fellow" to NM02Press@mail.house.gov by 2026-03-25. No calls or drop-ins, please.
equal_opportunity: The office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, national origin, disability, military status, age, marital status, or parental status.

description:
The Office of Congresswoman Eleanor Holmes Norton provides seasonal internship opportunities to undergraduate and graduate students interested in gaining congressional work experience in her Capitol Hill Office and in her District Office.

These tasks give students valuable, direct interaction with the inner workings of Capitol Hill and the legislative process.
responsibilities: Answer telephones
Open and sort mail
Organize Capitol tours to constituents
Track legislation
Attend community events
Assist with constituent casework
Coordinate press-related activities
Attend hearings
Prepare constituent correspondence
qualifications: Enrolled in or recently graduated from a post-secondary institution
Available for a minimum three-month commitment
Ability to work in person
DC ties preferred
Candidates from diverse backgrounds encouraged to apply
how_to_apply: To be considered for an internship in Congresswoman Norton's Capitol Hill office, email a one-page cover letter, resume, writing sample (2 pages max.), and at least one letter of recommendation to Raya.Kenney@mail.house.gov with the subject line: 'Norton Hill Office Summer 2026 Internship Application - First Name Last Name'. To be considered for an internship in Congresswoman Norton's District Office, email the same materials to dc00.nortonresume@mail.house.gov with the subject line: 'Norton District Office Summer 2026 Internship Application - First Name Last Name'. If applying for both offices, please do so in separate emails.
equal_opportunity: The office is an equal opportunity employer; we do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, gender identity or expression, or sexual orientation.

description: The District Office of Representative Joseph Morelle, NY-25 in Rochester, NY, will be accepting applications for an in-person internship during the Summer Semester 2026. Applications will be accepted March 3rd - April 3rd, 2026. Interns will learn about the functions of a congressional office, congressional communications, and the legislative process.
responsibilities: Completing constituent calls
Writing letters, certificates, and proclamations
Day-to-day office work
Interacting with members of staff in the Rochester office and virtually in the D.C. office
Other duties delegated by members of staff including various constituent case work or work on District-based projects of importance
qualifications: Excellent communication skills
Strong attention to detail
Ability to work in a fast-paced environment
Good sense of humor
New York State ties are preferred but not required
Current undergraduate students and current graduate students
how_to_apply: Interested applicants should submit the following in one PDF document: Resume, Cover letter, One-page writing sample, Dates of availability. To apply for the Spring 2026 District Office Internship, please fill out the google form found here: https://forms.gle/bipy9tt71qn5uuN6A. Our office will be accepting applications on a rolling basis until April 3rd at 11:59pm EST. Applicants are encouraged to submit their application early. For more information on the district internship please visit: https://morelle.house.gov/congressional-internships
equal_opportunity: The office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, disability, military status, age, marital status, or parental status.

office: Unresolved: New York Congressional Office (Rochester District)
description:
In the Washington, D.C. office, internships run throughout the year based on the semester calendar. Applicants must be based in Washington, D.C. for the duration of the internship.

Our internship program typically runs during these times:
Spring: Mid-January to mid-May; application window opens in September
Summer: Mid-May to mid-August; application window opens in February
Fall: Early September to mid-December; application window opens in June

Interns in the Washington, D.C. office play an important role in the daily operations of the office and gain firsthand experience in the legislative process. Throughout the semester, interns learn about the legislative process and the many other functions of a congressional office.

Full-time paid internships run Monday through Friday from 9:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m. when Congress is in session, and 9:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. when not in session (refer to the House Calendar). Part-time internships are scheduled to accommodate students' course schedules, as well as the needs of the office. Part-time interns can expect to receive either pay or academic credit.
responsibilities: Greeting guests and assisting with front office operations
Answering phones, recording constituent opinions, and correctly processing other inquiries
Conducting Capitol tours for constituents and other guests
Supporting the Legislative Correspondent with sorting mail and drafting constituent letters
Attending hearings and briefings, as requested by senior staff
Researching legislation for the Member and legislative team
Assisting with day-to-day office work and other duties as delegated by members of the staff
Interacting with members of staff in the D.C. office and virtually with the Rochester office
Other administrative tasks as assigned
Compile daily press clips and monitor media coverage of issues relevant to the Member
Draft press releases, media advisories, and statements under staff guidance
Assist with social media content creation and scheduling
Help update the office website and other digital platforms
Maintain press contact lists and track media inquiries
Support logistics for press events and prepare briefing materials for interviews or appearances
Assist with processing and tracking incoming invitations to the Member
Confirm details and logistics for meetings, events, and travel
Help maintain the Member's calendar and coordinate with district staff as needed
Prepare briefing materials for scheduled events or meetings
Support logistics for events, including visitor coordination and follow-up
Assist with schedule adjustments, conflicts, and communications
Track and organize inbound constituent correspondence
Draft responses to constituent letters on legislative issues and policy questions under staff supervision
Research issues raised by constituents to inform responses
Assist the Legislative Correspondent in preparing reports summarizing constituent concerns and trends
qualifications: Excellent written and verbal communications skills
Strong attention to detail
Ability to succeed in a fast-paced work environment
Good sense of humor
New York State ties are preferred but not required
Must be a U.S. citizen and legally eligible to work for the U.S. federal government
Current undergraduate students, current graduate students, and recent college graduates
how_to_apply: To apply for the Summer 2026 DC Internship, please fill out the google form found here: Summer 2026 DC Internship Application Google Form. Our office will be accepting applications on a rolling basis until April 3rd at 11:59 p.m. EST. Applicants are encouraged to submit their application early.
salary_info: Paid internship; part-time may receive pay or academic credit
equal_opportunity: The office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, disability, military status, age, marital status, or parental status.

office: Democratic Staff of the House Committee on Veterans' Affairs
description: The Democratic Staff of the House Committee on Veterans' Affairs seeks a press/digital intern for the Summer 2026 semester. A strong work ethic, the ability to excel in fast-paced environments, and a willingness to assist wherever needed is a must. The ideal candidate will have strong oral and written communications skills, be detail-oriented, have a familiarity with social media platforms, and have a desire to help veterans and learn about the Committee and the legislative process. This paid, full-time or part-time in-person position is based in Washington, D.C. The Committee strongly encourages veterans, minority, women, people with disabilities, LGBTQ+ candidates, and HBCU and MSI students to apply.
responsibilities: Compiling and distributing morning press clips
Tracking media hits for Committee members
Assisting with video shoots
Assisting with creating messaging packets and digital toolkits for the caucus
Writing first drafts of remarks, press releases, and video scripts
Developing social media content
Clipping floor and hearing remarks from members
Providing administrative support, including answering calls and delivering correspondence
Other tasks as assigned
qualifications: Strong work ethic
Ability to excel in fast-paced environments
Willingness to assist wherever needed
Strong oral and written communications skills
Detail-oriented
Familiarity with social media platforms
Desire to help veterans and learn about the Committee and the legislative process
how_to_apply: Please visit https://democrats-veterans.house.gov/opportunities for the application. The deadline to apply is 5 pm ET on Friday, 2026-03-20. Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis. Late applications will not be accepted.
equal_opportunity: The Committee strongly encourages veterans, minority, women, people with disabilities, LGBTQ+ candidates, and HBCU and MSI students to apply.

description:

description: The District Office of Representative Joseph Morelle, NY-25 in Rochester, NY, will be accepting applications for an in-person internship during the Summer Semester 2026. Applications will be accepted 2026-03-03 - 2026-04-03. Interns will learn about the functions of a congressional office, congressional communications, and the legislative process. Ideal candidates should possess excellent communication skills, strong attention to detail, the ability to work in a fast-paced environment, and a good sense of humor. New York State ties are preferred but not required. Internships are intended for current undergraduate students and current graduate students. description:
The office of Congressman Juan Ciscomani (AZ-06) is accepting applications for Summer 2026 paid internships in our Washington D.C. Office.

Ideal candidates are motivated, detail-oriented, and possess a strong work ethic, with the ability to communicate effectively with constituents and staff. This internship offers a valuable opportunity to gain hands-on experience in public service and constituent relations while learning about the day-to-day operations of a congressional office.
responsibilities: Answering phones and responding to constituent inquiries
Greeting visitors and assisting with front office operations
Leading guided tours of the U.S. Capitol
Conducting legislative research and writing memos on policy and local issues for the Member and staff
Assisting with additional administrative and legislative tasks as needed
qualifications: Strong written and verbal communication skills
Willingness to learn and take initiative
Professional demeanor and excellent attention to detail
Ability to work both independently and collaboratively as part of a team
Demonstrated interest in public service, government, or public policy
Arizona residency or connection to the 6th Congressional District preferred
how_to_apply: Interested applicants should submit a resume and brief cover letter outlining their interest and availability to william.wang@mail.house.gov
